relations: Leonardo da Vinci created the Mona Lisa, which is one of the most recognizable paintings in the world
Leonardo da Vinci was a key figure of the Renaissance, contributing significantly to its artistic achievements
The Mona Lisa has influenced the course of art history and continues to captivate audiences, showcasing the power of artistic expression
Michelangelo painted the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel, including the Creation of Adam and the Last Judgment
Michelangelo's work, especially in the Sistine Chapel, is a hallmark of Renaissance art
Michelangelo painted the ceiling of the chapel in the Vatican, a masterpiece that continues to inspire awe
Caravaggio painted "The Calling of Saint Matthew", a work known for its dramatic use of light and shadow
Claude Monet lived and painted in Giverny, where he created his famous water lilies series
Pablo Picasso was a pioneer of the Cubism movement, revolutionizing modern art
Picasso created "Les Demoiselles d'Avignon", a painting that is considered a pivotal work in modern art
Vincent van Gogh contributed to the art world with his emotionally charged landscapes, influencing the perception of art
Frida Kahlo's powerful self-portraits have left a significant mark on the art world, showcasing her unique vision and creativity
